# ToolkitFree - Full Capability Reference

ToolkitFree is a static Astro site with React interactive tools. Image processing runs in the browser. The selected file content is not sent to ToolkitFree for processing.

The local-file statement does not mean the page makes no network requests. Pages load site assets and Google AdSense. Background Remover downloads WebAssembly and model resources before local inference.

## Shared limits

There is no server upload-size quota because files are not uploaded for processing. Practical limits still exist: available device memory, image dimensions, browser decoder support, Canvas limits, and PDF generation memory. Large images or batches may be slow or fail, especially on mobile devices.

## Image Converter

Explicitly supported inputs: JPG/JPEG, PNG, WebP.
Explicitly supported outputs: JPG/JPEG, PNG, WebP.
Batch input: yes.

GIF, BMP, and SVG inputs use the browser decoder and are not guaranteed across devices. GIF conversion creates a static output from one decoded frame; animation is not preserved. SVG external resources and some features may not render.

HEIC and TIFF decoding are not implemented. GIF output is not implemented. Their explanatory URLs are marked noindex and are not listed in the sitemap until real support exists.

PNG output uses lossless encoding for decoded pixels, but converting from JPG cannot restore detail already lost in JPG compression. JPG and WebP outputs are re-encoded and may lose quality.

## Image Compressor

Supported inputs follow browser decoding, with JPG, PNG, and WebP as the documented baseline. Batch input is supported.

The tool re-encodes through Canvas and can optionally reduce dimensions. Output may be smaller, unchanged, or larger than the source. PNG quality controls do not create a guaranteed optimization. Reducing dimensions removes pixels and is not lossless.

The Compress to 100KB page is guidance only. The current implementation does not search for or guarantee an exact target size, so that page is marked noindex pending the dedicated target-size implementation.

## Image Resizer

Processes multiple images with shared dimension and format settings. Output uses Canvas. Browser memory and Canvas dimension limits apply.

## Image Cropper

Processes one image at a time. Outputs PNG, JPG, or WebP. PNG encoding is lossless for the cropped pixels; reducing the crop or converting to a lossy format changes data.

## Image to PDF

Explicitly supported image inputs are JPG, PNG, and WebP. Other formats depend on browser decoding and are not guaranteed. HEIC and TIFF are not supported.

Multiple images can be combined into one PDF with one image per page. Available memory and image dimensions limit practical batch size.

## Favicon Generator

Processes one image at a time and creates PNG favicon sizes plus a web manifest in a ZIP archive.

## Background Remover

Processes one image at a time. Model and runtime files are downloaded, then inference and image processing occur locally. Output quality depends on subject separation and image complexity.

## QR Generator

Creates one QR code at a time and exports PNG or SVG. QR payload data is generated locally; the page still loads normal website resources.

## Public wording policy

Use local file processing or no file upload for the file-content guarantee. Do not claim complete privacy, no network requests, unlimited file size, guaranteed compression percentage, guaranteed exact target size, or support for formats not implemented and verified.
